content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

Refactors the proposer proving pipeline by moving dispatch, collection, recovery, submit, and lifecycle responsibilities into focused proposer module types. `ProvingPipeline` now coordinates separate dispatcher and collector loops instead of owning the full proving state machine in one file, reducing `pipeline.rs` and keeping retry/restart handling with the component that owns it.

### Notes to reviewers

The functional surface stays inside `base-proposer`; public exports in `lib.rs` were intentionally adjusted for the split. `PipelineConfig` was folded into `DriverConfig`, `ProofCollectorRecoveryProvider` and `CollectedProof` were removed, and dispatcher runtime/tick result helper types are no longer exported. There are no remaining in-workspace users of those removed items.

The main review path is the interaction between `ProvingPipeline::run`, `ProofDispatcher`, `ProofCollectorOrchestrator`, `ProofRecovery`, and `PipelineHandle`.

### How has it been tested?

- `cargo test -p base-proposer`

### What changed? Why?

This cleans up output proposer proof submission by making proposal-proof verification an explicit `OutputProposer` requirement instead of a default internal-error implementation.

It also simplifies aggregate proof submission by passing the game address and proof bytes directly to `AggregateProofSubmitter::verify_proposal_proof`, which removes the extra `VerifyProposalProofSubmission` wrapper type.

The tests now reuse shared proposer test utilities and keep output proposer coverage focused on submission errors.

### Notes to reviewers

None.

### How has it been tested?

cargo test -p base-proposer -p base-proof-submission

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer config validation by returning `eyre::Result` directly instead of a local `ConfigError` enum.

Builds retry config inline, keeps dry-run sourced from the CLI flag while making signing and transaction manager config optional in dry-run, and updates config tests to parse minimal real CLI arguments.

### Notes to reviewers

The validation behavior is intended to stay the same while removing the extra error wrapper and duplicated test setup.

### How has it been tested?

`cargo test -p base-proposer config`

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer and proof submission error handling by consolidating error variants and updating callers to use the cleaned-up error types.

### Notes to reviewers

The diff is limited to proposer error cleanup and proof submission error conversion updates.

### How has it been tested?

- `cargo check -p base-proposer -p base-proof-submission`

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies the proposer admin JSON-RPC setup by registering the three admin methods directly on a jsonrpsee RpcModule. This removes the proc macro generated server trait, the custom AdminServer wrapper, and the reqwest-based integration test dependency while preserving the existing admin_startProposer, admin_stopProposer, and admin_proposerRunning methods.

### Notes to reviewers

The service now keeps the jsonrpsee ServerHandle returned by ProposerAdminApiServerImpl::spawn and performs shutdown directly through that handle.

### How has it been tested?

cargo test -p base-proposer

### What changed? Why?

Cleans up the TEE registrar verifier wiring by removing the local verifier wrapper and using the Nitro verifier types directly. This also trims now-unused registrar errors and adjusts certificate/service call sites to match the simpler verifier API.

### Notes to reviewers

Most of the diff is deletion in the registrar verifier module. The contract helper change keeps Nitro enclave verifier revocation calls aligned with the cleaned-up registrar flow.

### How has it been tested?

- RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar -p base-proof-contracts

## Summary
- document Boundless max-price gas-headroom behavior and standalone ramp override semantics
- make BoundlessProver construction reject asymmetric or inverted offer price configs
- revalidate offer price invariants before applying request params so direct field mutation cannot submit invalid min/max pairs

## Tests
- `DOCS_RS=1 RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-nitro-attestation-prover --features prove --lib`
- `DOCS_RS=1 RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar-bin --bin base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `DOCS_RS=1 RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar --lib`

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer CLI argument definitions and config mapping by using clap `Args`, typed defaults, and parser-level `NonZeroUsize` for recovery scan concurrency. Renames admin arg fields to match the flattened CLI names and constructs the admin socket address directly in config.

### Notes to reviewers

The recovery scan concurrency zero check moved from config validation to clap parsing through `NonZeroUsize`; runtime config still stores the value as `usize`.

### How has it been tested?

- `cargo test -p base-proposer`

### What changed? Why?

Removes the unused `RegisteredSigner` type and export from the registrar crate, along with the now-unused `rstest` dev dependency.

Keeps `InstanceHealthStatus` as a plain domain enum by moving AWS state mapping and registration eligibility checks to the call sites that use them.

### Notes to reviewers

Behavior should be unchanged: `initial` and `healthy` instances remain registerable, `draining` and `unhealthy` instances are skipped, and unknown AWS target states still map to `Unhealthy`.

### How has it been tested?

- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar` (39 passed)
- A plain `cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar` failed before tests because local `xcrun` could not find the Metal compiler utility.

## Summary
- Add a run-local last-known-active signer cache for registrar discovery cycles.
- Preserve cached signers and mark their instance unresolved when an instance disappears from otherwise successful discovery output.
- Evict cached missing instances after `INSTANCE_CACHE_TTL_CYCLES` so real removals eventually cancel proofs and deregister signers.
- Add driver regression tests for missing-instance protection, TTL eviction, TTL reset on refresh, and cold-start empty discovery behavior.

## Trade-off
- The 5-cycle TTL reduces transient discovery flakes causing proof cancellation or orphan deregistration.
- If the TTL is too short, partial discovery glitches can still leak through.
- If the TTL is too long, genuine instance removal cleanup is delayed.

## Testing
- `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo clippy -p base-proof-tee-registrar --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`
- Local caveat: exact cargo commands without `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1` fail on this machine because `risc0-sys` invokes `xcrun metal`, and the `metal` developer tool is not installed or not on PATH.
commit_subjects: fix(registrar): clarify cache ttl on resolve errors
fix(registrar): keep cached signers active on resolve error
fix(registrar): update cache tests for endpoint mock

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Stacked on the EVM-integration foundation (#3653). This PR makes EIP-8130 **account-management** transactions actually execute end-to-end in the EVM.

It wires `base-execution-eip8130` (previously an orphaned library) into the EVM through a gas-free, journal-backed `Eip8130Executor`, invoked from the alloy `Evm::transact_raw` seam — the path the block executor uses. For an EIP-8130 transaction the executor bypasses the mainnet single-frame handler and runs the enshrined pre-call pipeline directly against the block journal:

1. **Authorize** sender, payer, and ordered config changes (`TransactionAuthorizer`).
2. **Nonce** validate at inclusion and advance the 2D nonce — protocol (basic-account) bump, channel increment, or expiring-nonce mark (`NonceValidator` / `NonceManagerStorage`).
3. **Intrinsic gas** under the EIP-8130 schedule (`IntrinsicGas`), with the first-use and auto-delegation hints.
4. **Fee** cap validation + payer balance check, then debit the payer (`FeeCheck`).
5. **Apply** account changes (config/create/delegation) and install the deferred code effects — created bytecode, delegation indicators, and code-less-EOA auto-delegation to `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT` (...
commit_subjects: feat(eip8130): enshrined pre-call execution pipeline (account-management txns)

draft.title: feat(eip8130): enshrined pre-call execution pipeline (account-management txns)

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer driver lifecycle control by removing the type-erased `ProposerDriverControl` trait and wiring the admin RPC and service directly to `PipelineHandle`. The handle now tracks the running pipeline task in its mutex-protected session state instead of maintaining separate atomic running state.

### Notes to reviewers

The admin `admin_proposerRunning` method is now async because `PipelineHandle::is_running` reads the session state under the async mutex. This also removes the standalone admin mock test that existed only for the erased trait path; driver lifecycle coverage remains colocated with the handle implementation.

### How has it been tested?

- `cargo test -p base-proposer`

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer and proof submission error handling by consolidating error variants and updating callers to use the cleaned-up error types.

### Notes to reviewers

This PR is stacked on refactor/clean-driver.

### How has it been tested?

Not run locally.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

- Removes the proposer constants module and uses the shared constants directly where needed.
- Updates proposer CLI, pipeline, and service wiring to reference constants without the deleted re-export layer.

### Notes to reviewers

- No intended behavior change; this is scoped to proposer constants cleanup.

### How has it been tested?

- Not run; not requested.

## Summary
- reject signer attestation responses unless the count exactly matches signer public keys
- keep mismatched instances active but unresolved so orphan deregistration remains protected while registration fails closed
- rename the registrar endpoint abstraction from `SignerClient` to `EnclaveEndpointClient` and document its relationship to the shared `EnclaveApiClient` RPC surface

## Test plan
- `cargo +nightly fmt --package base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`

## Notes
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1` is needed locally because `xcrun` cannot find `metal` when `risc0-sys` attempts to build Metal kernels.
- Attempted `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo clippy -p base-proof-tee-registrar --all-targets -- -D warnings`, but it timed out locally while compiling dependency `risc0-circuit-recursion` before reporting crate diagnostics.

## Summary
- Derives deterministic attestation nonces from registrar domain, registry address, and signer address.
- Requests signer-specific attestations with those deterministic nonces, so recovered Boundless proofs can be validated after registrar restart without persisted nonce state.
- Decodes the generated proof journal before `registerSigner` submission and verifies the proven nonce matches the deterministic nonce.
- Blocks proof recovery for the signer when the proof journal is undecodable or carries the wrong nonce, so the next attempt can produce valid attestation material.

## Tangible value
This adds nonce binding on top of the freshness checks while preserving stateless Boundless recovery. It prevents accepting attestations that do not carry the registrar's expected domain/signer/registry nonce, without rejecting recovered proofs solely because they were generated during an earlier registrar cycle.

## Tests
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `just check::clippy-affected-ci "origin/main"`

## Summary
- Adds a final registrar-side freshness check after proof generation and immediately before `TEEProverRegistry.registerSigner` calldata is submitted.
- Reuses the registrar configured max attestation age from the Boundless prover config.
- Blocks proof recovery for the signer when the final gate rejects stale or undecodable proof output, so the next attempt fetches/generates a fresh proof instead of reusing the same bad one.

## Tangible value
This prevents the registrar from knowingly submitting a proof that is already doomed to fail the on-chain `MAX_AGE` check. It protects against recovered `Locked` proofs that age out while fulfillment completes, fresh proofs that become stale during proof/receipt/tx retries, and future proof providers returning old or malformed journals.

## Tests
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`

Note: the first local test attempt without `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1` failed before compiling this crate because the machine lacks the macOS `metal` developer tool required by `risc0-sys`.
commit_subjects: merge main into registrar proof age gate
fix(registrar): recheck proof freshness on retry

open.title: Prevent stale attestation proofs before registrar submission

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- use exponential backoff for retryable registrar registration tx submission errors
- treat the existing tx retry delay config as the initial backoff delay
- include the selected retry delay in retry warning logs
- clarify CLI/config docs that the retry delay is now the initial delay

## Test plan
- `cargo +nightly fmt --package base-proof-tee-registrar --package base-proof-tee-registrar-bin`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar signer_manager::tests::register_signer`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo clippy -p base-proof-tee-registrar --all-targets -- -D warnings`
commit_subjects: fix(registrar): back off tx submission retries

open.title: fix(registrar): back off tx submission retries

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Adds local `TEEProverRegistry` custom-error selectors to the contract binding.
- Exposes a small helper to decode known registry revert data to names like `AttestationTooOld`, `AttestationVerificationFailed`, `InvalidPublicKey`, and `PCR0NotFound`.
- Uses the decoded name in registrar registration errors when `TxManagerError::ExecutionReverted` carries registry revert bytes.

## Tangible value
This prevents on-call and operators from seeing only a generic `execution reverted` or raw selector bytes when the contract already returned a precise reason. Different errors imply different actions: `AttestationTooOld` means fetch/generate fresh proof material, `AttestationVerificationFailed` points at verifier/proof output, `InvalidPublicKey` or `PCR0NotFound` points at malformed attestation journal data, and `ImageHashReadFailed` points at registry/factory configuration.

## Tests
- `cargo test -p base-proof-contracts`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar signer_manager`
commit_subjects: chore(registrar): keep decoded reverts in logs

opened.title: Prevent stale attestation proofs before registrar submission

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Adds local `TEEProverRegistry` custom-error selectors to the contract binding.
- Exposes a small helper to decode known registry revert data to names like `AttestationTooOld`, `AttestationVerificationFailed`, `InvalidPublicKey`, and `PCR0NotFound`.
- Uses the decoded name in registrar registration errors when `TxManagerError::ExecutionReverted` carries registry revert bytes.

## Tangible value
This prevents on-call and operators from seeing only a generic `execution reverted` or raw selector bytes when the contract already returned a precise reason. Different errors imply different actions: `AttestationTooOld` means fetch/generate fresh proof material, `AttestationVerificationFailed` points at verifier/proof output, `InvalidPublicKey` or `PCR0NotFound` points at malformed attestation journal data, and `ImageHashReadFailed` points at registry/factory configuration.

## Tests
- `cargo test -p base-proof-contracts`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar signer_manager`
commit_subjects: chore(registrar): keep decoded reverts in logs

## Summary
- use exponential backoff for retryable registrar registration tx submission errors
- treat the existing tx retry delay config as the initial backoff delay

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Add a run-local last-known-active signer cache for registrar discovery cycles.
- Preserve cached signers and mark their instance unresolved when an instance disappears from otherwise successful discovery output.
- Evict cached missing instances after `INSTANCE_CACHE_TTL_CYCLES` so real removals eventually cancel proofs and deregister signers.
- Add driver regression tests for missing-instance protection, TTL eviction, TTL reset on refresh, and cold-start empty discovery behavior.

## Trade-off
- The 5-cycle TTL reduces transient discovery flakes causing proof cancellation or orphan deregistration.
- If the TTL is too short, partial discovery glitches can still leak through.
- If the TTL is too long, genuine instance removal cleanup is delayed.

## Testing
- `cargo +nightly fmt --all`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo clippy -p base-proof-tee-registrar --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`
- Local caveat: exact cargo commands without `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1` fail on this machine because `risc0-sys` invokes `xcrun metal`, and the `metal` developer tool is not installed or not on PATH.
commit_subjects: fix(registrar): clarify cache ttl on resolve errors
fix(registrar): keep cached signers active on resolve error
fix(registrar): update cache tests for endpoint mock

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

This cleans up output proposer proof submission by making proposal-proof verification an explicit `OutputProposer` requirement instead of a default internal-error implementation.

It also simplifies aggregate proof submission by passing the game address and proof bytes directly to `AggregateProofSubmitter::verify_proposal_proof`, which removes the extra `VerifyProposalProofSubmission` wrapper type.

The tests now reuse shared proposer test utilities and keep output proposer coverage focused on submission errors.

### Notes to reviewers

None.

### How has it been tested?

cargo test -p base-proposer -p base-proof-submission

## Summary
- Derives deterministic attestation nonces from registrar domain, registry address, and signer address.
- Requests signer-specific attestations with those deterministic nonces, so recovered Boundless proofs can be validated after registrar restart without persisted nonce state.
- Decodes the generated proof journal before `registerSigner` submission and verifies the proven nonce matches the deterministic nonce.
- Blocks proof recovery for the signer when the proof journal is undecodable or carries the wrong nonce, so the next attempt can produce valid attestation material.

## Tangible value
This adds nonce binding on top of the freshness checks while preserving stateless Boundless recovery. It prevents accepting attestations that do not carry the registrar's expected domain/signer/registry nonce, without rejecting recovered proofs solely because they were generated during an earlier registrar cycle.

## Tests
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `just check::clippy-affected-ci "origin/main"`

## Summary

Stacked on the EVM-integration foundation (#3653). This PR makes EIP-8130 **account-management** transactions actually execute end-to-end in the EVM.

It wires `base-execution-eip8130` (previously an orphaned library) into the EVM through a gas-free, journal-backed `Eip8130Executor`, invoked from the alloy `Evm::transact_raw` seam — the path the block executor uses. For an EIP-8130 transaction the executor bypasses the mainnet single-frame handler and runs the enshrined pre-call pipeline directly against the block journal:

1. **Authorize** sender, payer, and ordered config changes (`TransactionAuthorizer`).
2. **Nonce** validate at inclusion and advance the 2D nonce — protocol (basic-account) bump, channel increment, or expiring-nonce mark (`NonceValidator` / `NonceManagerStorage`).
3. **Intrinsic gas** under the EIP-8130 schedule (`IntrinsicGas`), with the first-use and auto-delegation hints.
4. **Fee** cap validation + payer balance check, then debit the payer (`FeeCheck`).
5. **Apply** account changes (config/create/delegation) and install the deferred code effects — created bytecode, delegation indicators, and code-less-EOA auto-delegation to `DEFAULT_ACCOUNT` (...
commit_subjects: feat(eip8130): enshrined pre-call execution pipeline (account-management txns)

opened.title: feat(eip8130): enshrined pre-call execution pipeline (account-management txns)

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer and proof submission error handling by consolidating error variants and updating callers to use the cleaned-up error types.

### Notes to reviewers

The diff is limited to proposer error cleanup and proof submission error conversion updates.

### How has it been tested?

- `cargo check -p base-proposer -p base-proof-submission`

## Summary
- reject signer attestation responses unless the count exactly matches signer public keys
- keep mismatched instances active but unresolved so orphan deregistration remains protected while registration fails closed
- rename the registrar endpoint abstraction from `SignerClient` to `EnclaveEndpointClient` and document its relationship to the shared `EnclaveApiClient` RPC surface

## Test plan
- `cargo +nightly fmt --package base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar`

## Notes
- `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1` is needed locally because `xcrun` cannot find `metal` when `risc0-sys` attempts to build Metal kernels.
- Attempted `RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo clippy -p base-proof-tee-registrar --all-targets -- -D warnings`, but it timed out locally while compiling dependency `risc0-circuit-recursion` before reporting crate diagnostics.

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer and proof submission error handling by consolidating error variants and updating callers to use the cleaned-up error types.

### Notes to reviewers

This PR is stacked on refactor/clean-driver.

### How has it been tested?

Not run locally.

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer driver lifecycle control by removing the type-erased `ProposerDriverControl` trait and wiring the admin RPC and service directly to `PipelineHandle`. The handle now tracks the running pipeline task in its mutex-protected session state instead of maintaining separate atomic running state.

### Notes to reviewers

The admin `admin_proposerRunning` method is now async because `PipelineHandle::is_running` reads the session state under the async mutex. This also removes the standalone admin mock test that existed only for the erased trait path; driver lifecycle coverage remains colocated with the handle implementation.

### How has it been tested?

- `cargo test -p base-proposer`

### What changed? Why?

- Removes the proposer constants module and uses the shared constants directly where needed.
- Updates proposer CLI, pipeline, and service wiring to reference constants without the deleted re-export layer.

### Notes to reviewers

- No intended behavior change; this is scoped to proposer constants cleanup.

### How has it been tested?

- Not run; not requested.

### What changed? Why?

Simplifies proposer config validation by returning `eyre::Result` directly instead of a local `ConfigError` enum.

Builds retry config inline, keeps dry-run sourced from the CLI flag while making signing and transaction manager config optional in dry-run, and updates config tests to parse minimal real CLI arguments.

### Notes to reviewers

The validation behavior is intended to stay the same while removing the extra error wrapper and duplicated test setup.

### How has it been tested?

`cargo test -p base-proposer config`

## Summary
- document Boundless max-price gas-headroom behavior and standalone ramp override semantics
- make BoundlessProver construction reject asymmetric or inverted offer price configs
- revalidate offer price invariants before applying request params so direct field mutation cannot submit invalid min/max pairs

## Tests
- `DOCS_RS=1 RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-nitro-attestation-prover --features prove --lib`
- `DOCS_RS=1 RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar-bin --bin base-proof-tee-registrar`
- `DOCS_RS=1 RISC0_SKIP_BUILD_KERNELS=1 cargo test -p base-proof-tee-registrar --lib`

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- gate log backfill readiness on the actual backfill window instead of the first post-handoff timestamp
- preserve SafeDB entries up to the current pending safe head when EL sync starts, truncating only data after that point
- add coverage for the backfill readiness boundary

## Testing
- mise exec -- go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop ./op-node/rollup/driver -count=1

## Rollout note
SN3 still needs a pullable CI-published image from this PR. A locally pushed dev image was rejected by the node service account, and local credentials cannot upload directly to the production images repo.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Reduces the interop verification activity readiness backoff from 1s to 100ms when chains are not yet ready for the next timestamp.

The error backoff remains 2s so persistent failures do not hammer logs or repeatedly retry expensive error paths.

## Rationale

The wait backoff is only a throttle after a no-progress verification round. It is not part of the verification correctness model, and the previous 1s delay added avoidable latency before noticing that chain data became available.

## Tests

- go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop

## Summary
- gate log backfill readiness on the actual backfill window instead of the first post-handoff timestamp
- preserve SafeDB entries up to the current pending safe head when EL sync starts, truncating only data after that point
- add coverage for the backfill readiness boundary

## Testing
- mise exec -- go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop ./op-node/rollup/driver -count=1

## Rollout note
SN3 still needs a pullable CI-published image from this PR. A locally pushed dev image was rejected by the node service account, and local credentials cannot upload directly to the production images repo.
